children.heading: .—Scope
children.label: a .—Scope
children.text: This section applies to any article or commodity tendered by a producer or vendor for sale or lease to the General Services Administration or to any procurement authority acting under the direction and control of the Administrator of General Services pursuant to this subtitle.
determine whether an article or commodity conforms to prescribed specifications and standards; or
aid in the development of specifications and standards.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator shall charge the producer or vendor a fee for the tests.

children.heading: .—Amount of fee if tests predominantly serve interest of producer or vendor
children.label: 2 .—Amount of fee if tests predominantly serve interest of producer or vendor
children.text: If the Administrator determines that conducting the tests predominantly serves the interest of the producer or vendor, the Administrator shall fix the fee in an amount that will recover the costs of conducting the tests, including all components of the costs, determined in accordance with accepted accounting principles.

children.heading: .—Amount of fee if tests do not predominantly serve interest of producer or vendor
children.label: 3 .—Amount of fee if tests do not predominantly serve interest of producer or vendor
children.text: If the Administrator determines that conducting the tests does not predominantly serve the interest of the producer or vendor, the Administrator shall fix the fee in an amount the Administrator determines is reasonable for furnishing the testing service.
